Details:
Removal of benzene by adsorptive media can be quite effective and is dependent on the contaminant and media properties (chemical structure, porosity, surface area, etc.). One bench-scale equilibrium study showed a removal range of 86 to 96 percent for three types of commercially available macroreticular resins with varying properties. Another study investigating bench and field-scale columns containing surfactant-modified zeolite clays found benzene removal in the range of 94 to 99 percent, although it should be noted that breakthrough was quite rapid.
April 2009
